title: Apple’s failed self-driving car program left a legacy of powerful AI chips
summary: Apple's abandoned self-driving car project inadvertently led to the development of the Neural Engine, which became the foundation for the company's on-device AI capabilities. The Neural Engine, debuting in iPhone X with the A11 Bionic, powered crucial computer vision features like Face ID and Animoji. This legacy demonstrates how failed projects can yield breakthrough technologies in unexpected areas.
content:
Apple's self-driving car program never really got off the ground, but it may have been what made the company's chips the powerful AI performers they are. Early in the development of the self-driving platform, Apple realized that it would need powerful on-device AI processing. While the car processor was never finished, as Mark Gurman details in his latest Power On newsletter, it did lead to the development of the Neural Engine, the backbone of Apple's on-device AI processing. 
The Neural Engine made its debut with the iPhone X and the A11 Bionic. In those early days, it was primarily used for computer vision, powering FaceID, Animoji, and a …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: 苹果失败的自动驾驶汽车项目留下强大AI芯片的遗产
zh.summary: 苹果放弃的自动驾驶汽车项目意外地促成了神经引擎的开发，该引擎成为公司设备端AI能力的基础。神经引擎在搭载A11仿生芯片的iPhone X中首次亮相，支撑了Face ID和Animoji等关键计算机视觉功能。这个遗产证明了失败的项目可能在意想不到的领域产生突破性技术。

title: TechCrunch Mobility: A robotaxi ultimatum
summary: TechCrunch Mobility covers the rapidly evolving transportation landscape, particularly the growing importance of AI in robotaxi development. This segment explores critical decisions and ultimatums shaping the autonomous vehicle industry.
content: Welcome back to TechCrunch Mobility, your hub for the future of transportation and now, more than ever, how AI is playing a part.

zh.title: TechCrunch出行：自动驾驶出租车的最后通牒
zh.summary: TechCrunch出行覆盖了快速演变的交通运输格局，特别是AI在自动驾驶出租车开发中日益增长的重要性。本栏目探讨了塑造自动驾驶汽车行业的关键决策和最后通牒。

title: One of SteelSeries’ best gaming headsets is over $100 off
summary: SteelSeries is discounting its Arctis Nova Pro Wireless gaming headset to $239.99 due to damaged packaging, despite the hardware remaining unused and undamaged. The headset, which originally launched four years ago, remains competitive with features including active noise cancellation, swappable batteries, and multi-platform compatibility. At this price point, it represents significant value for high-end wireless gaming audio.
content:
The SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro gaming headset. | Photo by Amelia Holowaty Krales	

SteelSeries has the Arctis Nova Pro Wireless gaming headset on sale for $239.99 (currently between $300 and $350 at other retailers). The Xbox version that supports a host of other platforms including PlayStation, PC, and Switch is available in both black and white. The PlayStation version, which supports the same platforms except for Xbox, is discounted in white. Both versions can also connect to mobile devices via Bluetooth.
SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Wireless
The Nova Pro Wireless gaming headset supports active noise cancellation, swappable batteries, Bluetooth support, and compatibility with PC and most consoles — including Xbox and PlayStation. This model includes a base station that makes it easy to toggle crucial sound settings. Read our review.

Where to Buy:

 $379.99 $239.99 at SteelSeries (Xbox, black, blemished box)
 $379.99 $239.99 at SteelSeries (Playstation, white, blemished box)
 $379.99 $239.99 at SteelSeries (Xbox, white, blemished box)

This isn’t your average price reduction; SteelSeries’ reason for discounting the headsets is because their packaging is damaged. It assures that the hardware inside is still unused and undamaged, and the headset includes the same one-year warranty as any of its new headsets.
The Nova Pro Wireless was an impressive gaming headset when it launched four years ago — and still is — especially at this price point. It has all the features you’re probably expecting from a high-end wireless headset, like active noise cancellation for blocking out sounds, a noise-canceling microphone for clear callouts and crisp voice delivery, and Bluetooth connectivity in case you need to take a call. In addition, the slick USB base station adds a ton of unique functionality, like charging a spare batteries to minimize downtime when one dies, onboard controls for game profiles and sound mixing, and the ability to blend up to three inputs at a time (includi

zh.title: 赛睿最佳游戏耳机之一降价超过100美元
zh.summary: 赛睿因包装受损将其Arctis Nova Pro Wireless游戏耳机折扣至239.99美元，尽管硬件保持未使用和完好状态。这款耳机四年前发布，凭借主动降噪、可更换电池和多平台兼容性等功能仍具竞争力。以此价格点，它为高端无线游戏音频提供了显著价值。

title: How Philips Hue got the smart home right
summary: Philips Hue has come closer than most products to achieving the ideal smart home experience of universal control and seamless adaptation. The Verge's Version History episode explores how Hue's design philosophy overcomes typical smart home fragmentation and complexity. The company's approach demonstrates that effective smart home integration is achievable without major infrastructure changes.
content:
A photo of a lightbulb glowing purple. | Photo: Amelia Holowaty Krales / The Verge	

The state of the smart home can be frustrating, because it is just so obvious how things ought to work. You should be able to control everything from everywhere. Your spaces should adapt to what you're doing and how you're feeling. Making your home smart shouldn't require renovating, and the smarts should be mostly invisible. All of this is, of course, incredibly hard to pull off - but the goal is pretty clear.
Until now, maybe no product has come closer to nailing the smart home than Philips Hue. And on this episode of Version History, we dig into all the things Hue got right. The Verge's David Pierce and Jennifer Pattison Tuohy are joined …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: 飞利浦Hue是如何实现完美智能家居的
zh.summary: 飞利浦Hue比大多数产品更接近实现理想的智能家居体验，提供通用控制和无缝适配。The Verge的版本历史节目探讨了Hue的设计理念如何克服典型智能家居的碎片化和复杂性。该公司的方法证明了在无需重大基础设施改变的情况下实现有效的智能家居集成是可行的。

title: Less is more with the Oura Ring 5
summary: The Oura Ring 5 positions itself as an excellent casual health tracker and the best smart ring on the market, though it may disappoint those seeking detailed fitness metrics. Coming less than two years after the Ring 4, the Ring 5 introduces incremental improvements in software and sensors that don't warrant an upgrade for recent users. The device represents the smart ring market's evolution toward broader appeal over specialized fitness tracking.
content:
The cross stitch pattern is by NathNolu on Etsy.	

If you're reading an Oura Ring 5 review at The Verge, you likely fall into one of two camps: newcomers looking for a smartwatch alternative, or Oura users pondering an upgrade. In the case of the former, this is a great casual health tracker and the best smart ring on the market - but not your best choice if you really sweat the fitness details. If you fall into the latter, you don't need to upgrade.
I say this because the Oura Ring 4 came out less than two years ago. The ceramic version, of which I am a big fan, came out less than a year ago. These were major updates over the Oura Ring Gen 3, both in terms of software and in terms of senso …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: Oura Ring 5：少即是多
zh.summary: Oura Ring 5将自己定位为一款出色的休闲健康追踪器和市场上最好的智能戒指，尽管它可能会让寻求详细健身指标的用户感到失望。在Ring 4发布不到两年后推出的Ring 5在软件和传感器方面带来了增量改进，但这些改进不足以值得最近用户升级。该设备代表了智能戒指市场从专业健身追踪向更广泛吸引力发展的演变趋势。

title: Blue Prince became a bonding — and learning — experience for my family
summary: A father describes how gaming, particularly Blue Prince, has become a meaningful bonding experience with his 11-year-old son, shifting from passive observation to active co-play. The article explores gaming's role in family relationships and intergenerational connection.
content:
My wife and son spent their weekends exploring and piecing together the mansion in Blue Prince. | Image: Courtesy of Raw Fury	

I've always been the gamer in the family. When my son was born nearly 11 years ago, gaming was one of the things I looked forward to sharing with him. Pulling up a chair next to me, he would watch as I played Sea of Thieves with friends, often wearing the too-big-for-his-head headset to talk with them, instructing me where to go. Now instead of him watching me play through Clair Obscur, I'm sitting next to him and he battles Calamity Ganon in Breath of the Wild. His real gaming passion for years has been Minecraft - he appreciates the rules and order, as well as the calming environment of creative mode. He also likes to meticulously stack up …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: 蓝王子：我家人的联系纽带与学习之旅
zh.summary: 一位父亲讲述了游戏，尤其是《蓝王子》如何与他11岁的儿子建立了有意义的联系，从被动观看转变为主动参与。文章探讨了游戏在家庭关系和代际连接中的作用。

title: The fight against AI data centers is just beginning
summary: Communities are mounting growing resistance to AI data center expansion, driven by concerns about local power consumption and resource strain. The article traces this activism back to 2015 with Apple's data center in Ireland, contextualizing current protests nationwide.
content:
A yard sign opposing a planned data center is displayed along Route 54 in Mount Carmel Township Northumberland County. | Image: Getty Images	

This is The Stepback, a weekly newsletter breaking down one essential story from the tech world. For more on the data center buildout, follow Emma Roth. The Stepback arrives in our subscribers' inboxes on Sunday at 8AM ET. Opt in for The Stepback here.
How it started
Years before the AI boom threatened local power grids, a small group of protesters set the stage for the battles cropping up across communities today. In 2015, Apple announced plans to build a roughly $1 billion data center in the sleepy town of Athenry, Ireland. The data center's 500-acre site would power Apple's services in Europe, including iTunes, iMessage, and Siri. At th …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: 抵抗人工智能数据中心的战役才刚刚开始
zh.summary: 社区对人工智能数据中心扩张的抵抗日益增长，主要源于对本地电力消耗和资源压力的担忧。文章追溯了这一行动主义到2015年苹果公司在爱尔兰的数据中心，为当前全国范围内的抗议活动提供了背景。

title: The real mystery behind Moana: After 1,700 years, why did Polynesians suddenly sail east?
summary: New climate evidence offers insights into why Polynesians resumed long-distance eastward voyages after a 1,700-year gap, challenging historical understanding of Pacific exploration patterns. The discovery helps explain a significant pause in the region's maritime history.
content: New climate evidence adds context to these long voyages.

zh.title: 莫阿纳背后的真相：1700年后，波利尼西亚人为何突然向东航海？
zh.summary: 新的气候证据揭示了为什么波利尼西亚人在1700年的间隙后恢复了远程东向航海，这挑战了人们对太平洋探险模式的历史理解。这一发现有助于解释该地区海事历史中一个重要的停顿期。

title: Reed Jobs would rather talk about curing cancer than his last name
summary: Reed Jobs' venture firm Yosemite has grown to 17 employees and is capitalizing on patent expirations of blockbuster drugs while integrating AI into its biotech operations. The firm has experienced rapid expansion since its founding, positioning itself at the intersection of biotech innovation and artificial intelligence applications in the sector.
content: When we last sat down with Jobs at TechCrunch Disrupt nearly three years ago, his firm Yosemite was brand new and biotech was still reeling from its post-pandemic crash. Now, the venture outfit has a team of 17; a cluster of blockbuster drugs are all losing patent protection in roughly the same window, creating all kinds of new opportunities; and AI has gone from a curiosity to, in Jobs's words, a huge part of what Yosemite does. "I didn't expect Yosemite to be moving this fast," he said.

zh.title: 里德·乔布斯宁愿谈论治愈癌症，也不愿谈论他的姓氏
zh.summary: 里德·乔布斯的风险投资公司约塞米蒂已发展到17名员工，正在利用畅销药物的专利过期期限，同时将人工智能整合到其生物技术业务中。该公司自成立以来经历了快速扩张，将自己定位在生物技术创新和人工智能在该领域应用的交点。
